product.name: Arox T1 Static Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Tkaniny robocze funkcyjne — antystatyczne i hydrofobowe
product.categorySourceGroup: Polyester Woven Fabrics for Raincoats, Jackets and Coveralls
product.specLine: 110 g/m², 99% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/2 S Twill Fabric
descriptions: 99% polyester 1% carbon, antistatic ESD workwear twill fabric.
Arox T1 Static pro fabrics are mostly used for clean room's cloths.
Suitable for antistatic ESD application like electronic industry, certified for EN ISO 1149-5.
110 g/m², 99% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/2 S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp > 400N ; Weft > 300N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp > 20N ; Weft > 15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Antistatic property alone does not establish energy-sector use per exclusion rules; ESD/electronics context is closer to industrial cleanroom than energy utilities.
Described for 'clean room's cloths' which could relate to controlled environments including pharma/medical, but no explicit medical/healthcare use is stated.

evidence: Mostly used for clean room's cloths
Suitable for antistatic ESD application like electronic industry
Category source group: Polyester Woven Fabrics for Raincoats, Jackets and Coveralls
primary.rationale: The fabric is an antistatic ESD workwear textile intended for cleanroom/industrial protective garments (coveralls, cloths). No narrower named sector such as energy, medical, or electronics manufacturing is explicitly stated as the dominant commercial use—only generic ESD/cleanroom workwear, which fits general industrial protective apparel best.

secondary.rationale: No independently supported secondary application; 'clean room' reference is too generic to establish medical use, and antistatic property alone cannot establish energy per exclusion rules.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Antistatic property alone does not establish energy-sector use per rule 8; no explicit electrical utility or high-voltage context stated.
Cleanroom mention is generic and not explicitly tied to healthcare/medical use.
